original_abstract: The monograph represents a significant milestone in shaping the patterns of official communication in Poland. The research material comprises office collections originating from Upper Silesia during the 16th to 18th centuries, encompassing multiple towns governed by European municipal law. Due to the broad jurisdiction of local offices, the extant documents embody two distinct discourses: chancellery discourse, governing legal-social and ownership relations among residents, and court discourse, concerned with arbitrating disputes and maintaining public order. The characteristics of both discourses are related to the description of pragmatic functions typical for them, text categories and genres of statements recorded in written form.
humanized_abstract: This monograph is a very important step in the development of communicative patterns in official Polish language. The source material for this study consists of office collections of documents coming from Upper Silesia in the 16th – 18th centuries, including several towns subject to various European municipal laws. Due to the wide scope of authority of local offices, the existing sources of administrative documents contain two distinct styles of language use: the chancellery discourse, which regulated the official communication of citizens in relation to legal-social and property matters, and the court discourse, which dealt with settling disputes and maintaining public order. The features of these discourses are linked to the characteristics of the pragmatic functions they were supposed to perform, as well as to the text categories and genres of written statements contained in the sources.

The features of these discourses are linked to the characteristics of the pragmatic functions they were supposed to perform, as well as to the text categories and genres of written statements contained in the sources.
